summary: Deliver revisioned Worker snapshots and read-model deltas to the client
acceptance_criteria: Boot supplies a schema-versioned read-model snapshot and each accepted authoritative change supplies monotonically revisioned deltas from the Worker.
Rejected commands, previews, local selection, animation, and renderer interaction never create authoritative deltas.
The client applies a delta only when session, schema version, base revision, and next revision match; duplicates and stale deltas are ignored deterministically.
A revision gap, reconnect, incompatible schema, or invalid delta triggers an explicit full-snapshot resynchronization rather than local repair or silent success.
Client state contains bounded serializable read models, pending request metadata, and UI state only; it never retains full WorldState or grants Pixi/React mutation authority.
Applying the same snapshot and ordered delta stream yields identical read-model hashes in browser and Node contract tests.
required_tests: property tests cover ordered deltas, duplicate delivery, stale session, revision gaps, schema mismatch, snapshot recovery, and browser/Node hash parity
notes: Read models are purpose-built protocol views, not serialized WorldState aliases.
Shared shell and Worker transport files remain owned by their dependency tasks.
